mad_librarian.name: The Mad Librarian
mad_librarian.description: You have more papyrus than a mummy factory. You spent three Ages in a basement staring at tablets while your neighbors were actually building a civilization.

pythagoras_understudy.name: Pythagoras’ Understudy
pythagoras_understudy.description: Math is your only friend. While others are building armies, you’re calculating the square root of 'Winning.'

gear_grinder.name: The Gear Grinder
gear_grinder.description: You never met a mechanical symbol you didn't like. You're probably hoping to build a robot to play the rest of the game for you.

tablet_titan.name: Tablet Titan
tablet_titan.description: You collected so many tablets that your city basically invented the internet in the Bronze Age. Too bad there's no Wi-Fi.

alchemy_dropout.name: Alchemy Dropout
alchemy_dropout.description: You tried to turn lead into gold, but you mostly just turned cards into Science symbols. At least the lab looks cool.

shield_waller.name: The Shield Waller
shield_waller.description: Your city is less 'metropolis' and more 'spiked cage.' Your military score is the only thing keeping you from a total loss.

siege_engine.name: The Siege Engine
siege_engine.description: You don't just win conflicts; you make sure your neighbors feel the shame of their -1 tokens for generations.

mercenary_mogul.name: The Mercenary Mogul
mercenary_mogul.description: You buy your way into wars. You’re the reason military budgets exist, and the reason your neighbors are broke.

spartan_wannabe.name: Spartan Wannabe
spartan_wannabe.description: Who needs a Palace when you have a very sharp stick? You have zero Civilian points but won every conflict.

reluctant_pacifist.name: The Reluctant Pacifist
reluctant_pacifist.description: You built one Guard Tower in Age I and then just... stopped. You're watching the carnage with a 'Safety First' badge.

palace_dweller.name: The Palace Dweller
palace_dweller.description: You live for the prestige. Your city is beautiful, useless, and worth a mountain of points. Just like a real government.

statue_stalker.name: Statue Stalker
statue_stalker.description: You have a statue for every occasion. We get it, you're pretty. Now please pass the clay.

blue_blood.name: The Blue Blood
blue_blood.description: You find Science 'too messy' and Military 'too loud.' You prefer the quiet dignity of a 7-point Senate.

civic_superfan.name: Civic Super-Fan
civic_superfan.description: You’ve built every public building available. Your city has the highest taxes and the best-trimmed hedges.

pantheon_planner.name: Pantheon Planner
pantheon_planner.description: You’re building for the gods. Hopefully, they appreciate the 5 points, because your neighbors certainly don't.

ill_just_buy_it_guy.name: The 'I'll Just Buy It' Guy
ill_just_buy_it_guy.description: You produce nothing but gold. Your treasury score is huge, and you bought every single resource from your neighbors.

marketplace_manipulator.name: Marketplace Manipulator
marketplace_manipulator.description: You control the flow of goods. If your neighbors want a brick, they have to go through you—and your 2-coin fee.

gold_hoarder.name: The Gold Hoarder
gold_hoarder.description: You have so many coins you’re using them as wallpaper. Points are points, but shiny things are forever.

tavern_tourist.name: Tavern Tourist
tavern_tourist.description: You spent most of Age I in the pub. You didn't get much done, but you had a great time doing it.

resource_refugee.name: The Resource Refugee
resource_refugee.description: You literally produce nothing. You’re a professional shopper in a world of producers.

guild_ghost.name: The Guild Ghost
guild_ghost.description: You’re invisible until Age III, then you swoop in and steal 18 points based entirely on your neighbor's hard work.

copycat_king.name: Copycat King
copycat_king.description: You don't have a strategy. You just have neighbors with good strategies that you’re currently stealing.

wonder_worker.name: The Wonder Worker
wonder_worker.description: You finished your Wonder in Age II while everyone else was still figuring out how to make glass. Show off.

foundation_fanatic.name: Foundation Fanatic
foundation_fanatic.description: You spent the whole game on Stage 1 of your Wonder. It’s a very nice base, though. Very sturdy.

pyramid_scheme.name: Pyramid Scheme
pyramid_scheme.description: You’re building a pyramid. It’s expensive, it’s pointless, but it’s worth a massive pile of points.

laboratory_rat.name: The Laboratory Rat
laboratory_rat.description: You spent Age I, II, and III in a basement. You emerged blinking into the light with 48 points and a headache.

observatory_overlord.name: Observatory Overlord
observatory_overlord.description: You’re looking at the stars while your neighbor is looking at your lack of shields. Hope the constellations can block a catapult.

patent_pending.name: The Patent Pending
patent_pending.description: You have a lot of great ideas, but none of them involve actually helping your neighbors with their resources.

scroll_soul.name: Scroll Soul
scroll_soul.description: Your city smells like old parchment and desperation. You’d trade your firstborn for one more School.

geometry_giant.name: Geometry Giant
geometry_giant.description: You see the world in shapes. Specifically, the shape of three matching symbols that give you 7 points.

wall_to_wall_warrior.name: Wall-to-Wall Warrior
wall_to_wall_warrior.description: If there’s a brick on the table, it’s going into a fortification. Your city is essentially a giant, angry LEGO set.

peace_treaty_tease.name: The Peace-Treaty Tease
peace_treaty_tease.description: You look like you’re going for Blue cards until the very last turn, when you drop a Circus and ruin everyone’s day.

iron_fist.name: The Iron Fist
iron_fist.description: You rule with a heavy hand and a lot of shields. Diplomacy is just something you do while reloading the ballista.

spearhead_specialist.name: Spearhead Specialist
spearhead_specialist.description: You’re always the first to draft a red card. It’s not about the points; it’s about sending a message.

phalanx_fanatic.name: The Phalanx Fanatic
phalanx_fanatic.description: You love a good formation. Your city is so organized it’s scary, mostly because of all the spears.

gardens_architect.name: Gardens Architect
gardens_architect.description: Your city is basically a giant park. It’s very relaxing until you realize you can't eat flowers or fight with thorns.

town_crier.name: The Town Crier
town_crier.description: You love making announcements. Usually, it's just 'Look how many Blue cards I have!' followed by smug silence.

altar_ego.name: Altar Ego
altar_ego.description: You’ve built so many altars that you’re legally a deity in three different ancient provinces.

fountain_finder.name: The Fountain Finder
fountain_finder.description: You’re obsessed with water features. Your city is 40% fountains and 60% people trying not to slip.

senate_sweeper.name: Senate Sweeper
senate_sweeper.description: You’ve dominated the political landscape. You have a seat at every table, mostly so you can talk about your points.

bazaar_brawler.name: Bazaar Brawler
bazaar_brawler.description: You fight for every coin. You’ll spend ten minutes arguing over a 1-coin discount and you bring a sword to the market.

the_middleman.name: The Middleman
the_middleman.description: You’re the bridge between your neighbors. You take a little off the top of every single transaction.

coin_collector.name: Coin Collector
coin_collector.description: You treat every coin like a beloved pet. It’s hard to spend them when they’re so cute.

export_expert.name: The Export Expert
export_expert.description: You’re always sending stuff away for profit. Your city is empty, but your pockets are full.

trade_route_ruler.name: Trade Route Ruler
trade_route_ruler.description: You’ve established routes to everywhere. You’re basically the ancient world’s version of a modern logistics company.

the_parasite.name: The Parasite
the_parasite.description: Your entire score is based on what the people next to you built. You’re like a high-scoring barnacle.

opportunist_supreme.name: Opportunist Supreme
opportunist_supreme.description: You see an opening, you take it. Especially if that opening is a Guild card that scores off your rich neighbors.

shadow_broker.name: The Shadow Broker
shadow_broker.description: You’re pulling the strings from the shadows. Or just drafting the Spies Guild and taking credit for everything.

purple_prose.name: Purple Prose
purple_prose.description: You write your own victory. Usually involving a lot of math based on how many brown cards your neighbor has.

colossus_king.name: Colossus King
colossus_king.description: You’re building a giant statue. It’s big, it’s bold, and combined with your military might, it’s winning.

base_051.name: The Architect
base_051.description: Obsessed with leaving a legacy. You prioritized completing your Wonder and building monumental civic structures.

base_052.name: The Mercenary
base_052.description: War is a business, and business is booming. You funded your military campaigns with a strong commercial backbone.

base_053.name: The Bureaucrat
base_053.description: A master of logistics and city planning. You focused on civic scoring backed by a sprawling trade network.

base_054.name: The Tycoon
base_054.description: Cash is king. You monopolized trade routes and hoarded coins to buy your way to victory.

base_055.name: The Artisan
base_055.description: A master craftsman. You leaned heavily on Guilds to capitalize on the achievements of your neighbors.

base_056.name: The Diplomat
base_056.description: You avoided the bloodshed entirely, focusing instead on a perfectly balanced, peaceful civilization.

base_057.name: The Alchemist
base_057.description: Transforming resources into pure progress. You aggressively pursued scientific advancements while hoarding the resulting wealth.

base_058.name: The Imperialist
base_058.description: Expansion through force and culture. You marched your armies forward while building a sprawling, beautiful city behind them.

base_059.name: The Populist
base_059.description: A leader of the people. You focused entirely on civic happiness and a booming economy to keep the citizens content.

base_060.name: The Pioneer
base_060.description: Always looking forward. You paired high scientific achievement with solid civic infrastructure.

base_061.name: The Strategist
base_061.description: Calculated and precise. You maintained an incredibly even spread across all scoring categories without a single glaring weakness.

base_062.name: The Opportunist
base_062.description: You played the table, not just your board. You relied on Commercial cards and late-game Guilds to siphon points based on your neighbors' strategies.

base_063.name: The Monopolist
base_063.description: You controlled the resources. By dominating the yellow cards, you forced your neighbors to pay you while raking in trade points.

base_064.name: The Ascetic
base_064.description: You ignored material wealth entirely, scraping by with zero coins but achieving massive scientific and wonder milestones.

base_065.name: The Warlord
base_065.description: Pure, unadulterated aggression. You prioritized the highest possible military score at the expense of almost everything else.

base_066.name: The Visionary
base_066.description: You built a city of the future. High science, a fully constructed wonder, and the guilds to back up your unique approach.

base_067.name: The Quartermaster
base_067.description: You supplied the war effort. A unique blend of military might and the commercial infrastructure required to sustain it.

base_068.name: The Patrician
base_068.description: Old money and high society. You dominated the blue civic cards and purple guilds for a highly cultured victory.

base_069.name: The Pragmatist
base_069.description: No flashy science or massive armies, just a highly efficient engine of commerce, coins, and civic structures.

base_070.name: The Syndicate
base_070.description: You ran the underground. High guilds, high commerce, and enough military to keep your neighbors intimidated.

base_071.name: The Traditionalist
base_071.description: You played the classics. A fully built wonder, a strong civic presence, and a solid standing army.

base_072.name: The Polymath
base_072.description: A renaissance player. You dabbled heavily in science while maintaining an impressive array of guilds and civic structures.

base_073.name: The Scavenger
base_073.description: You capitalized on what was left over. A chaotic but effective mix of low-tier military wins, leftover science, and coins.

base_074.name: The Benefactor
base_074.description: You funded the city's greatness. Massive treasury scores combined with towering civic monuments.

base_075.name: The Autocrat
base_075.description: Total control. You finished your wonder early, built a formidable army, and heavily taxed your citizens via commercial cards.

leader_001.name: The Figurehead
leader_001.description: You let your leaders do the heavy lifting. A massive score from high-value leader cards carried an otherwise mediocre city.

leader_002.name: The Generalissimo
leader_002.description: Led from the front lines. You recruited military masterminds to guarantee absolute supremacy in every conflict.

leader_003.name: The Patron of the Arts
leader_003.description: You sponsored the great thinkers and artists of the age, turning your city into a cultural beacon dripping with prestige points.

leader_004.name: The Headmaster
leader_004.description: You recruited history's greatest minds. Your leaders fueled a massive scientific engine that left the rest of the world in the dark ages.

leader_005.name: The Guild Master
leader_005.description: A networking genius. You used specific leaders to discount and maximize the power of a sprawling network of specialized Guilds.

leader_006.name: The Plutocrat
leader_006.description: You bought your way to the top. Your leaders generated an absurd amount of wealth, which you converted directly into a massive treasury score.

leader_007.name: The Dynasty
leader_007.description: Generations of great leadership. You maintained a perfectly balanced empire heavily subsidized by the sheer power of your ruling class.

leader_008.name: The Benefactor
leader_008.description: You relied on leaders who discounted building costs, allowing you to construct towering civic monuments and commercial hubs on a budget.

leader_009.name: The Wonder Builder
leader_009.description: You drafted architects and engineers. Your leaders were entirely dedicated to finishing your Wonder faster and better than anyone else.

leader_010.name: The Demagogue
leader_010.description: A populist leader focused on war and wealth. You used your authority to extort your neighbors and dominate the battlefield.

leader_011.name: The High Priest
leader_011.description: A city led by faith and culture. You ignored the military entirely, using your leaders to build an awe-inspiring, wealthy, and civilian-heavy utopia.

leader_012.name: The Diplomat
leader_012.description: You carefully navigated the political landscape. By using leaders to secure key resources, you played a flexible, highly opportunistic game.

leader_013.name: The Innovator
leader_013.description: A leader in technology. You paired brilliant scientific minds with a strong commercial backbone to fund their research.

leader_014.name: The Taskmaster
leader_014.description: You squeezed every ounce of productivity out of your people. High commercial scoring, maxed-out guilds, and leaders who demanded results.

leader_015.name: The Warmonger
leader_015.description: You hired mercenaries and tacticians to fuel an endless crusade. Your leaders were weapons, and your military score proves it.

leader_016.name: The Monopoly
leader_016.description: You recruited the ancient world's best merchants. A staggering commercial score heavily subsidized by your cunning leadership.

leader_017.name: The Polymath
leader_017.description: Led by true renaissance thinkers. You generated points from an incredibly diverse array of science, guilds, and civilian monuments.

leader_018.name: The Scion
leader_018.description: Born into greatness. You utilized leaders who practically built your Wonder for you, cementing your legacy early in the game.

leader_019.name: The Visionary
leader_019.description: You saw the future. Your leaders paved the way for a massive scientific breakthrough that your neighbors couldn't hope to match.

leader_020.name: The Puppet Master
leader_020.description: You pulled the strings from the shadows. High leader points combined with opportunistic guilds to piggyback off everyone else's success.

leader_021.name: The Conqueror
leader_021.description: Total military dominance backed by the wealth to sustain it. You drafted leaders who made your armies cheap and deadly.

leader_022.name: The Administrator
leader_022.description: Your leaders created a highly efficient bureaucracy. Massive civilian scores combined with just enough trade to keep the city running.

leader_023.name: The Hoarder
leader_023.description: You drafted leaders who generated raw wealth. At the end of Age III, you sat on a mountain of coins while everyone else was broke.

leader_024.name: The Noble
leader_024.description: You surrounded yourself with high society. Pure prestige scoring driven entirely by blue cards and the white cards of your elite inner circle.

leader_025.name: The Monarch
leader_025.description: A terrifying blend of raw power and elite leadership. You built an aggressive military and finished your wonder, driven by legendary figures.

leader_026.name: The Aristocrat
leader_026.description: High society and deep pockets. You drafted leaders who elevated your status, leaning heavily on purple guilds and hoarded wealth.

leader_027.name: The Mercenary Captain
leader_027.description: You hired the best commanders coin could buy. A devastating military strategy funded directly by a massive treasury.

leader_028.name: The Oracle
leader_028.description: Guided by divine foresight. Your leaders helped you construct a magnificent Wonder while delving deeply into the sciences.

leader_029.name: The Syndic
leader_029.description: You controlled the board's economy through powerful figures. A dominant commercial engine supported by highly specialized guilds.

leader_030.name: The Philanthropist
leader_030.description: You used your elite status to build a paradise. Massive civilian monuments funded by a healthy treasury and guided by visionary leaders.

leader_031.name: The Warlord
leader_031.description: Led by tyrants and conquerors. You maxed out your military capabilities, letting your leaders drive your aggressive expansion.

leader_032.name: The Academic
leader_032.description: You assembled a brain trust. By recruiting the smartest minds in history, your scientific progress was completely unstoppable.

leader_033.name: The Archmage
leader_033.description: A rare and potent combination. You used your leaders to perfectly synchronize massive scientific gains with high-scoring guilds.

leader_034.name: The Vanguard
leader_034.description: You led the charge in both culture and combat. A balanced approach using leaders to secure military wins and civilian prestige.

leader_035.name: The Trade Baron
leader_035.description: Your leaders were economic savants. You completely monopolized the yellow cards and controlled the flow of resources.

leader_036.name: The Artisan
leader_036.description: You drafted master builders. Your leaders discounted your Wonder and fueled a massive late-game surge in guild points.

leader_037.name: The Chancellor
leader_037.description: A master of statecraft. Your leaders helped you maintain an incredibly balanced, resilient city that scored consistently across the board.

leader_038.name: The Hierophant
leader_038.description: A spiritual and cultural epicenter. Your leaders guided the construction of both your Wonder and towering civilian monuments.

leader_039.name: The Tactician
leader_039.description: A calculated, terrifying approach. You combined brilliant scientific leaders with a flawless, advanced military strategy.

leader_040.name: The Emissary
leader_040.description: You connected your people through trade and diplomacy. High commercial output backed by stunning civilian infrastructure and solid leadership.

leader_041.name: The Sovereign
leader_041.description: Absolute rule. You utilized a powerful inner circle of leaders to max out your military and finish your Wonder with ease.

leader_042.name: The Exarch
leader_042.description: A governor with an iron fist and deep pockets. You used leaders to maintain a terrifying military while monopolizing regional trade.

leader_043.name: The Financier
leader_043.description: The true power behind the throne. Your leaders were strictly business, generating immense wealth and a staggering commercial score.

leader_044.name: The Luminary
leader_044.description: A shining beacon of civilization. Your point total was completely carried by legendary figures and the sheer volume of your blue civilian cards.

leader_045.name: The Erudite
leader_045.description: A city of quiet, brilliant progress. You favored leaders who boosted your science and civilian infrastructure, ignoring war entirely.

leader_046.name: The Autarch
leader_046.description: You built it all yourself. Relying heavily on leaders who discounted building materials, you constructed a massive Wonder and an unbeatable army.

leader_047.name: The Sentinel
leader_047.description: A highly specialized defensive strategy. You used leaders to build a formidable military and backed it up with clever guild drafting.

leader_048.name: The Opportunist
leader_048.description: You let others do the work. Your leaders and highly active guilds allowed you to siphon incredible points off your neighbors' achievements.

leader_049.name: The Zealot
leader_049.description: Blind devotion to your leaders and your Wonder. You sacrificed science, commerce, and military just to see your great project completed.

leader_050.name: The Pantheon
leader_050.description: You collected practically every white card you could get your hands on. Your city wasn't much to look at, but your leaders were legends.

cities_001.name: The Shadow Broker
cities_001.description: You controlled the underworld. A massive score driven by black cards and commercial dominance, pulling the strings from the dark.

cities_002.name: The Extortionist
cities_002.description: You bled your neighbors dry. By heavily utilizing aggressive black cards, you forced everyone else into debt while you profited.

cities_003.name: The Pacifist
cities_003.description: You completely avoided the bloodshed. Using diplomacy icons on black cards, you hid behind your walls and focused on civic prestige.

cities_004.name: The Black Market Boss
cities_004.description: Your economy thrived in the shadows. You hoarded coins and utilized illegal trade routes to build an unassailable financial empire.

cities_005.name: The Syndicate
cities_005.description: A criminal empire operating in plain sight. You balanced respectable civic monuments with a terrifying array of black cards.

cities_006.name: The Assassin
cities_006.description: Silent and deadly. You used diplomacy to dodge major conflicts while utilizing specific black cards and guilds to target your opponents' weaknesses.

cities_007.name: The Spy
cities_007.description: You copied your neighbors' homework. Heavy use of black cards allowed you to mimic technologies and stay competitive in the arms race.

cities_008.name: The Corrupt Official
cities_008.description: You bent the rules to your advantage. A mix of civic planning and deep corruption yielded a beautifully compromised city.

cities_009.name: The Usurer
cities_009.description: You capitalized on others' misfortune. While everyone else was drowning in debt tokens, your treasury and city score skyrocketed.

cities_010.name: The Informant
cities_010.description: You sold secrets for survival. A high science output funded entirely by questionable dealings and the protection of the black market.

cities_011.name: The Traitor
cities_011.description: You turned on your own people. You accumulated a massive amount of debt yourself, but offset it by dragging your neighbors down with you.

cities_012.name: The Thief
cities_012.description: You stole your way to victory. Highly active in the underworld, you drained your opponents' resources to fund your own city's growth.

cities_013.name: The Smuggler
cities_013.description: You slipped through the cracks. Bypassing standard trade routes, you used black cards to supply your massive commercial empire.

cities_014.name: The Mercenary
cities_014.description: Loyalty to the highest bidder. You used black cards to bolster an aggressive, unstoppable military campaign.

cities_015.name: The Fence
cities_015.description: You dealt in stolen goods. A sprawling commercial network supported entirely by the illegal activities of the underworld.

cities_016.name: The Anarchist
cities_016.description: You wanted to watch the world burn. High debt, chaotic guild drafting, and purely destructive black cards.

cities_017.name: The Fugitive
cities_017.description: Always on the run. You used diplomacy to constantly evade battles, sacrificing your own treasury and taking on debt just to survive.

cities_018.name: The Defector
cities_018.description: You switched sides at the perfect moment. A balanced strategy that heavily relied on black cards to swing the momentum in the late game.

cities_019.name: The Underboss
cities_019.description: Second in command, but holding all the real power. You built your Wonder using the illicit resources generated by your black cards.

cities_020.name: The Safehouse
cities_020.description: A haven for the wicked. You focused heavily on finishing your Wonder and hiding behind diplomacy tokens to score peacefully.

cities_021.name: The Secret Society
cities_021.description: An enlightened cult operating in the shadows. You paired massive scientific discoveries with deeply ingrained black card mechanics.

cities_022.name: The Mastermind
cities_022.description: Every move was calculated. You used black cards not for chaos, but to surgically dismantle your opponents' strategies while advancing your own.

cities_023.name: The Provocateur
cities_023.description: You started wars you didn't have to fight. High military aggression combined with diplomacy meant you controlled exactly when and where combat happened.

cities_024.name: The Loan Shark
cities_024.description: You held everyone's purse strings. A staggering treasury score, fueled by a trade empire and forcing endless debt upon the board.

cities_025.name: The Diplomatic Immunity
cities_025.description: Untouchable. You ignored the military entirely, using black cards to completely shield your pristine, civilian-heavy city from any repercussions.

cities_026.name: The Embezzler
cities_026.description: You siphoned city funds into your own pockets. High treasury and black cards, leaving the city in massive debt while you got rich.

cities_027.name: The Fixer
cities_027.description: You solved problems behind closed doors. A balanced city built on a foundation of diplomacy and well-timed underworld favors.

cities_028.name: The Ghost
cities_028.description: You were barely there. By abusing diplomacy, you ghosted every military conflict, leaving your neighbors to fight while you built in peace.

cities_029.name: The Racketeer
cities_029.description: You ran a protection racket. A terrifying military presence combined with a commercial monopoly enforced by the black market.

cities_030.name: The Insurgent
cities_030.description: You funded a rebellion. Aggressive military tactics fueled by heavy debt and chaotic, disruptive city cards.

cities_031.name: The Defaulter
cities_031.description: You mortgaged your city's future. You built an incredible Wonder and civic structures, but drowned in debt tokens to get there.

cities_032.name: The Aristocrat
cities_032.description: Untouchable elite. You used diplomacy and city cards to insulate your high-society civilian structures from the wars of the commoners.

cities_033.name: The Shadow Cabinet
cities_033.description: A dark enlightenment. You paired a massive scientific engine with heavy investments in the underworld to protect your research.

cities_034.name: The Kingpin
cities_034.description: You ruled the streets. A massive score derived almost entirely from specialized guilds and heavy utilization of city cards.

cities_035.name: The Mole
cities_035.description: Deep cover espionage. You stayed poor and heavily in debt, but stole enough scientific and guild secrets to scrape together a victory.

cities_036.name: The Opportunist
cities_036.description: You profited off the chaos. While everyone else was taking on debt, you cleanly executed a strategy based on trade and black market exploitation.

cities_037.name: The Propagandist
cities_037.description: You controlled the narrative. High military aggression masked by beautifully constructed civic monuments and underworld connections.

cities_038.name: The Debt Collector
cities_038.description: You were the one knocking on the door. A flawless treasury score maintained by making sure your opponents paid dearly for every mistake.

cities_039.name: The Double Agent
cities_039.description: Nobody knew whose side you were on. An incredibly even spread of points across all categories, heavily lubricated by city cards.

cities_040.name: The Oligarch
cities_040.description: A corrupt corporatocracy. You completely monopolized commercial trade routes and used black cards to cement your financial dominance.

cities_041.name: The Deserter
cities_041.description: You abandoned your posts. Zero military, massive debts, but you managed to hide in the underworld long enough to build a functioning city.

cities_042.name: The Smear Campaigner
cities_042.description: You ruined your rivals' reputations. A heavy focus on blue cards and black cards to secure prestige while tearing others down.

cities_043.name: The Shadow Scholar
cities_043.description: Forbidden knowledge. You utilized illegal avenues to secure scientific breakthroughs that no one else dared to research.

cities_044.name: The War Profiteer
cities_044.description: Peace is bad for business. You fueled massive military conflicts and used commercial/black cards to sell weapons to the victors.

cities_045.name: The Cultist
cities_045.description: A dark devotion. You took on massive debt and utilized forbidden city cards entirely to speed the construction of your Wonder.

cities_046.name: The Rogue
cities_046.description: You played by your own rules. A highly aggressive military strategy funded by stolen coins and underworld connections.

cities_047.name: The Blacksmith
cities_047.description: You forged a shady empire. High guild scoring, a completed Wonder, and heavy reliance on the black market to get the materials you needed.

cities_048.name: The Bankrupt
cities_048.description: A cautionary tale. You leaned way too hard into the underworld, resulting in an overwhelming amount of debt that crippled your final score.

cities_049.name: The Exile
cities_049.description: Cast out from high society. You abandoned civic duty and science, focusing entirely on your Wonder, the black market, and taking on debt to survive.

cities_050.name: The Godfather
cities_050.description: The undisputed ruler of the city. You achieved a masterful balance of standard scoring while completely dominating the underworld mechanics.

edifice_001.name: The Chief Architect
edifice_001.description: You spearheaded every major communal project. A massive score from Edifice rewards paired perfectly with completing your own Wonder.

edifice_002.name: The Boycotter
edifice_002.description: You refused to help the community. You took on massive debts from failed Edifices, but used those saved resources to fuel a dominant science engine.

edifice_003.name: The Philanthropist
edifice_003.description: You built for the people. High participation in Edifices combined with towering civilian monuments for a beautiful, prestige-heavy city.

edifice_004.name: The Profiteer
edifice_004.description: You used communal projects strictly for personal gain. You hoarded the coins generated from Edifice rewards to build an unassailable treasury.

edifice_005.name: The Free Rider
edifice_005.description: You let everyone else do the heavy lifting. You ignored the Edifices completely, eating the debt while heavily investing in specialized Guilds.

edifice_006.name: The Garrison Commander
edifice_006.description: You used communal projects to arm your city. Participating in military-focused Edifices gave you the edge needed to crush your neighbors.

edifice_007.name: The Foreman
edifice_007.description: You organized the workforce. A highly active commercial sector that perfectly supported your continuous contributions to the Edifices.

edifice_008.name: The Saboteur
edifice_008.description: You deliberately let projects fail to hurt your neighbors. You took the debt hit yourself, but ruined everyone else's carefully laid plans.

edifice_009.name: The Mason
edifice_009.description: A master of stone and clay. You perfectly balanced constructing your own Wonder with securing every possible Edifice reward.

edifice_010.name: The Diplomat
edifice_010.description: A friend to all. You maintained an even spread across all standard categories while graciously contributing to every communal project.

edifice_011.name: The Opportunist
edifice_011.description: You only pitched in when it suited you. A mixed bag of partial Edifice rewards and a few debts, balancing communal effort with self-interest.

edifice_012.name: The Innovator
edifice_012.description: Progress above all. You used Edifice rewards strictly to subsidize a terrifyingly fast and effective scientific engine.

edifice_013.name: The Tycoon
edifice_013.description: You bought out the competition. Massive commercial dominance and high treasury, completely ignoring communal projects because you could afford the fines.

edifice_014.name: The Warlord
edifice_014.description: You viewed Edifices as a distraction from conquest. You took the penalties on the chin and poured everything into absolute military supremacy.

edifice_015.name: The Civic Leader
edifice_015.description: A shining example for the board. You led the charge on communal projects while building breathtaking civilian structures in your own city.

edifice_016.name: The Contractor
edifice_016.description: You supplied the materials for the world. A massive commercial trading network that ensured you could easily afford to participate in every Edifice.

edifice_017.name: The Outcast
edifice_017.description: You played an entirely solitary game. You practically abandoned the board's shared goals, scraping together a weird, guild-heavy strategy amid mounting debts.

edifice_018.name: The Aristocrat
edifice_018.description: You believed communal labor was beneath you. You focused purely on prestige and wealth, absorbing the debt tokens without blinking.

edifice_019.name: The Guardian
edifice_019.description: A protector of the realm. You contributed heavily to the Edifices, ensuring your city was well-fortified, respected, and prosperous.

edifice_020.name: The Bureaucrat
edifice_020.description: You managed the logistics of progress. High civic scoring backed by a relentless, highly efficient contribution to every shared project.

edifice_021.name: The Pragmatist
edifice_021.description: You only participated when the math worked out. A highly balanced, calculated game where you took a few debts to secure more lucrative standard cards.

edifice_022.name: The Guildmaster
edifice_022.description: You focused on specialized trades. Using the rewards from Edifices, you perfectly set up a devastating late-game run of purple cards.

edifice_023.name: The Embezzler
edifice_023.description: You stole from the project funds. You completely ignored the Edifices, letting them fail so your opponents took debt while you hoarded coins.

edifice_024.name: The Vanguard
edifice_024.description: Always the first to commit. You instantly jumped on every Edifice, securing the best rewards early and fueling a fast-paced, aggressive city build.

edifice_025.name: The Overachiever
edifice_025.description: You did it all. You fully completed your own Wonder, maxed out your Edifice participation, and still built a highly respectable city.

edifice_026.name: The Martyr
edifice_026.description: You took the fall for the city. You absorbed massive Edifice debts to ensure your civilization's scientific and military dominance remained untouched.

edifice_027.name: The Visionary Architect
edifice_027.description: You redefined the skyline. A perfectly synergized game where your Wonder and the shared Edifices were completed in record time.

edifice_028.name: The Recluse
edifice_028.description: You shut the gates and focused inward. You ignored the communal projects entirely, taking the debts while building an unassailable scientific utopia.

edifice_029.name: The Supplier
edifice_029.description: You controlled the materials. Your massive commercial network meant you could easily contribute to every Edifice while still raking in coins.

edifice_030.name: The Merciless
edifice_030.description: You had no time for teamwork. You maxed out your military to extort your neighbors, completely ignoring the Edifices and eating the penalties.

edifice_031.name: The Pacifist Builder
edifice_031.description: You refused to fight, pouring all your resources into communal Edifices and stunning blue civilian cards to secure a prestige victory.

edifice_032.name: The Industrialist
edifice_032.description: A well-oiled machine. You heavily utilized brown and gray cards to ensure you were always ready to claim the highest Edifice rewards.

edifice_033.name: The Isolationist
edifice_033.description: You built an empire in the mountains, disconnected from the world. High guilds and wonder scoring, but terribly deep in Edifice debt.

edifice_034.name: The Stormbringer
edifice_034.description: You brought chaos to the board. An aggressive, disruptive strategy that caused multiple Edifices to fail, hurting everyone including yourself.

edifice_035.name: The Night Owl
edifice_035.description: You worked late to get it done. You snatched up late-game Edifice rewards that your opponents couldn't afford, swinging the game in your favor.

edifice_036.name: The Golden Child
edifice_036.description: Everything you touched turned to points. Flawless participation in the Edifices backed by a perfectly balanced, wealthy civilization.

edifice_037.name: The Sovereign
edifice_037.description: You ruled with absolute authority. You dictated the flow of the game, effortlessly completing your Wonder, leading the military, and finishing the Edifices.

edifice_038.name: The Patrician
edifice_038.description: Old money and high culture. You ignored the grit of communal labor, comfortably paying the debt penalties while you focused on high society.

edifice_039.name: The Spark
edifice_039.description: You ignited an era of progress. You practically single-handedly funded the scientific and communal projects for the entire board.

edifice_040.name: The Warmonger
edifice_040.description: You burned it all down. You intentionally let the Edifices fail, capitalizing on your opponents' weakened states to crush them militarily.

edifice_041.name: The Syndicate
edifice_041.description: You organized the labor force. Heavy commercial and guild synergies allowed you to comfortably secure rewards from every shared project.

edifice_042.name: The Diplomatic Envoy
edifice_042.description: You bridged the gaps between nations. A highly respected city with massive civilian appeal and flawless participation in all communal goals.

edifice_043.name: The Anchor
edifice_043.description: You held the board together. When others faltered, you stepped up, ensuring the Edifices were completed while maintaining a balanced city.

edifice_044.name: The Keymaster
edifice_044.description: You unlocked the city's potential. An incredibly precise strategy where you took calculated debts to secure key Guilds and Wonder stages.

edifice_045.name: The Explorer
edifice_045.description: You looked beyond your borders. High scientific output and commercial trade, heavily subsidizing the communal Edifice projects.

edifice_046.name: The Vault Keeper
edifice_046.description: You hoarded the city's wealth. A staggering treasury score, practically ignoring the Edifices because you could afford the fines.

edifice_047.name: The Magistrate
edifice_047.description: You upheld the law. A perfectly balanced approach, ensuring your military was strong, your civilians happy, and the Edifices completed.

edifice_048.name: The Bankrupt Builder
edifice_048.description: You bit off more than you could chew. You tried to build your Wonder, fight wars, and do science, but ultimately drowned in Edifice debt.

edifice_049.name: The Grandmaster
edifice_049.description: A legendary performance. You perfectly optimized every single aspect of your city while effortlessly claiming every communal reward.

edifice_050.name: The Defaulter
edifice_050.description: You simply gave up on the communal effort. You focused strictly on a high-scoring Guild strategy and let the debt tokens pile up.

armada_001.name: The Admiral
armada_001.description: Master and commander of the seas. You dominated the naval conflict tokens while maintaining a formidable land army.

armada_002.name: The Explorer
armada_002.description: You charted the unknown. Pushing your green and blue fleets to the limit to discover high-value islands and scientific breakthroughs.

armada_003.name: The Corsair
armada_003.description: A feared pirate king. You used your red fleet to extort your neighbors and secure military victories on land and sea.

armada_004.name: The Merchant Marine
armada_004.description: You controlled the global supply chain. Massive commercial success on land, paired with a dominant yellow trading fleet.

armada_005.name: The Colonist
armada_005.description: You focused entirely on settling new lands. Your massive score boost was driven almost entirely by high-value Island cards.

armada_006.name: The Shipwright
armada_006.description: You built an awe-inspiring armada. The construction of your Wonder was directly tied to the rapid expansion of your dockyard tracks.

armada_007.name: The Navigator
armada_007.description: Perfectly balanced on the water. You advanced all your ships evenly, explored islands, and secured naval borders.

armada_008.name: The Privateer
armada_008.description: Sanctioned piracy. You used your naval conflicts to drain your opponents' treasuries while heavily advancing the yellow trade track.

armada_009.name: The Coast Guard
armada_009.description: You focused on defense. Your red fleet ensured no one could touch your shores while you built massive civilian monuments on land.

armada_010.name: The Oceanographer
armada_010.description: The sea is your laboratory. You ignored land military in favor of mapping the oceans and gathering scientific lore from islands.

armada_011.name: The Smuggler
armada_011.description: Operating in the shadows. You used the yellow fleet and clever commercial cards to move resources under the radar to exotic islands.

armada_012.name: The Commodore
armada_012.description: A high-ranking naval elite. You focused heavily on the blue fleet track to rack up massive prestige points at sea and on land.

armada_013.name: The Cartographer
armada_013.description: You mapped the world. A specialized strategy relying entirely on uncovering new islands and finishing your Wonder.

armada_014.name: The Freebooter
armada_014.description: Total chaos on the high seas. You neglected your city's infrastructure to maximize islands, naval conflicts, and your dockyard.

armada_015.name: The Trade Prince
armada_015.description: You cornered the international market. Your yellow fleet and commercial cards made you the undisputed economic ruler.

armada_016.name: The Conquistador
armada_016.description: You brought the sword to new shores. High land military, aggressive red fleet naval power, but very little actual exploration.

armada_017.name: The Portmaster
armada_017.description: Your city was the hub of the world. Huge civilian scores backed up by a bustling, fully developed harbor.

armada_018.name: The Whaler
armada_018.description: A focused, ruthless oceanic enterprise. You utilized your dockyard tracks and commercial lanes to scrape together a gritty win.

armada_019.name: The Castaway
armada_019.description: You practically abandoned your home city to live on the islands. A massive island score with barely any points on the mainland.

armada_020.name: The Grand Fleet
armada_020.description: Unstoppable naval supremacy. Your tracks were maxed out, ensuring massive point swings in dockyard and conflict scores.

armada_021.name: The Leviathan
armada_021.description: A monster in the water. You paired an intimidating military presence with specialized guilds and heavy naval conflict wins.

armada_022.name: The Mariner
armada_022.description: A true seafarer. You balanced the green scientific fleet with the yellow trading fleet, discovering islands and ignoring war.

armada_023.name: The Blockader
armada_023.description: You choked out the competition. Heavy naval conflict activity combined with a monopolistic hold on commercial resources.

armada_024.name: The Diplomatic Envoy
armada_024.description: You used your ships to connect, not conquer. High civilian scores and island exploration, proving that peaceful seas lead to prosperity.

armada_025.name: The Kraken
armada_025.description: You dragged your opponents to the depths. Massive land military, unmatched naval conflicts, and fully upgraded dockyards.

armada_026.name: The Wayfinder
armada_026.description: Guided by the stars. You focused heavily on completing your Wonder and discovering every Island you could find.

armada_027.name: The Blockade Runner
armada_027.description: You bypassed enemy fleets to deliver the goods. A highly lucrative commercial engine paired with just enough dockyard presence to survive.

armada_028.name: The Treasure Hunter
armada_028.description: X marks the spot. You sailed specifically to dig up wealth, hoarding coins and scoring massive points from remote island discoveries.

armada_029.name: The Imperial Fleet
armada_029.description: The pride of the empire. You neglected the islands entirely to build a massive, fully upgraded dockyard and an unstoppable military.

armada_030.name: The Pearl Diver
armada_030.description: You found beauty in the depths. High civilian monument scoring paired perfectly with uncovering pristine, untouched islands.

armada_031.name: The Sea Scholar
armada_031.description: A floating academy. You pushed the green ship track to its absolute limit, reaping massive scientific benefits from your naval expeditions.

armada_032.name: The Invader
armada_032.description: You launched amphibious assaults on your neighbors. An absolute menace in both land military and naval conflicts.

armada_033.name: The Castellan
armada_033.description: You heavily fortified your shores. A defensive strategy relying on a strong civilian foundation and just enough naval power to repel attackers.

armada_034.name: The Sea Dog
armada_034.description: A scrappy, opportunistic sailor. You grabbed whatever points you could find across guilds, islands, and minor naval skirmishes.

armada_035.name: The Archipelago
armada_035.description: You expanded your empire across multiple islands, using them to fuel a massive commercial and treasury-based victory.

armada_036.name: The Siren
armada_036.description: You lured your enemies to their doom. High civilian prestige masked a surprisingly deadly naval conflict strategy.

armada_037.name: The Shipwrecked
armada_037.description: Things on the mainland went horribly wrong, but you managed to salvage the game by isolating yourself on the islands.

armada_038.name: The Ironclad
armada_038.description: Slow, steady, and unbreakable. You pushed the red track and military while utilizing specific guilds to maximize your point output.

armada_039.name: The Flotilla
armada_039.description: A massive, peaceful fleet. You advanced all your non-military ship tracks equally, creating a beautifully balanced dockyard.

armada_040.name: The Poseidon
armada_040.description: Ruler of the waves. You practically ignored the base game cards entirely to achieve absolute dominance across the dockyard, islands, and naval conflicts.

armada_041.name: The Buccaneer
armada_041.description: You struck fast and faded away. Decent military strength backed by just enough naval conflict wins to keep your opponents poor.

armada_042.name: The Lighthouse Keeper
armada_042.description: You guided the way for others while focusing on your own city's Wonder and civic beauty, lightly exploring the closest islands.

armada_043.name: The Lookout
armada_043.description: Always scanning the horizon. You prioritized the scientific and commercial tracks to stay one step ahead of the competition.

armada_044.name: The Mutineer
armada_044.description: You overthrew the traditional strategies. You abandoned your Wonder entirely to focus on aggressive naval and land combat.

armada_045.name: The Custom House
armada_045.description: You taxed the seas heavily. A high commercial score paired with a fully realized dockyard board meant endless resources.

armada_046.name: The Tidal Wave
armada_046.description: You crushed your opponents under the weight of your fleets and armies, while your Wonder stood as a monument to your power.

armada_047.name: The Deep Sea Diver
armada_047.description: You delved into the unknown. An incredibly focused strategy aimed entirely at maximizing island exploration points and science.

armada_048.name: The Ferryman
armada_048.description: You connected the continents. A steady flow of commerce, civilian scoring, and a highly active dockyard kept you in the running.

armada_049.name: The Monsoon
armada_049.description: A chaotic force of nature. You drafted cards wildly, resulting in an unpredictable mix of high guilds, naval conflicts, and island finds.

armada_050.name: The Fleet Admiral
armada_050.description: A legendary commander. You successfully balanced a robust land empire with absolute perfection across all three Armada categories.

base_101.name: The Hermit
base_101.description: Knowledge is your only companion. You retreated into the sciences, completely ignoring the noise of the battlefield.

base_102.name: The Iron Duke
base_102.description: Strength through tradition. You built a formidable military force backed by towering, permanent civic monuments.

base_103.name: The High Priest
base_103.description: Your city is a temple. You focused entirely on completing your Wonder and elevating your civilian prestige to divine levels.

base_104.name: The Collector
base_104.description: You value unique achievements. Your strategy revolved around specialized guilds and the commercial infrastructure to pay for them.

base_105.name: The Spendthrift
base_105.description: Easy come, easy go. You draft high-value commercial cards but never manage to keep enough gold in the bank for a high treasury score.

base_106.name: The Oracle
base_106.description: You foresaw a future of progress and influence. A unique blend of massive scientific breakthroughs and high-scoring late-game guilds.

base_107.name: The Legatus
base_107.description: Conquest at all costs. You marched through the ages with an unbeatable army, sacrificing scientific growth for pure tactical dominance.

base_108.name: The Curator
base_108.description: You built a city for the ages. Your museum-like civilization focused on blue monuments and a fully realized, magnificent Wonder.

base_109.name: The Banker
base_109.description: Wealth is the ultimate victory. You cornered the commercial market and sat on the largest treasury in the ancient world.

base_110.name: The Weaver
base_110.description: You tied the table together. Using commercial trade and specialized guilds, you profited from every one of your neighbors' moves.

base_111.name: The Peasant
base_111.description: Humble but persistent. You focused on simple civic life, ignoring the complexities of high-level commerce and trade.

base_112.name: The Philosopher
base_112.description: A life of the mind. You achieved total scientific supremacy while maintaining a peaceful, non-militaristic stance.

base_113.name: The Diplomat
base_113.description: A friend to all. You maintained an incredibly balanced city, participating in every category without making any enemies.

base_114.name: The Architect
base_114.description: Master of blueprints. You focused on the structural integrity of your civilization, prioritizing your Wonder and civic monuments above all else.

base_115.name: The Mercenary
base_115.description: You fought for gold. Your strategy was simple: build a massive military and use commercial cards to ensure you stayed wealthy enough to keep it.

base_116.name: The Chronicler
base_116.description: Recording the rise of man. You balanced a deep interest in history and science with a large, record-keeping civilian bureaucracy.

base_117.name: The Artificer
base_117.description: Crafting greatness. You focused on the mechanical and structural, pairing your Wonder construction with specialized guild achievements.

base_118.name: The Usurer
base_118.description: Profit through interest. You focused on commercial cards that generated high wealth, using that gold to buy a top-tier treasury score.

base_119.name: The Hoplite
base_119.description: A soldier of the line. Your city was built on a foundation of military drills and discipline, ignoring the flowery guilds and commerce of others.

base_120.name: The Senator
base_120.description: A master of internal politics. You prioritized the internal civic strength of your city and the late-game influence of the guilds.

base_121.name: The Cartographer
base_121.description: Mapping the known world. You focused on exploration and trade, pairing a strong commercial network with high scientific progress.

base_122.name: The Steward
base_122.description: A careful manager of resources. You prioritized a high treasury and commercial stability, avoiding the high costs of a standing army.

base_123.name: The Centurion
base_123.description: Military and market power. You dominated the battlefield while ensuring your trade routes remained profitable and secure.

base_124.name: The Augur
base_124.description: Knowledge and mysticism. You pursued a unique path through high scientific research centered around the completion of your Wonder.

base_125.name: The Titan
base_125.description: A civilization of myth. You achieved the impossible: absolute military supremacy and a fully completed, world-renowned Wonder.
